title: Handlers
description: Writing handlers, job context, logging, and chaining

blocks.text: A handler is an async function that receives a JobContext and performs the job's work. Handlers are registered with hound.on() or via defineJob().
Every handler receives a ctx object with the job's metadata and utilities:

rows: Queue the job is running in
Append a log message to the job record

blocks.text: Call ctx.logger() to append timestamped messages to the job record. Logs are stored in the JobRecord and visible via the management API:
Logs are capped at maxLogsPerJob (default: 100) to keep storage bounded.
Handlers can emit downstream jobs using ctx.emit(), ctx.emitAsync(), or ctx.emitAndWait(). These behave identically to hound.emit() but are bound to the same Hound instance automatically:
If a handler throws, Hound marks the job as failed and re-enqueues it according to the retry configuration. The error message and stack are stored in the job record.
After all attempts are exhausted the job moves to the dead-letter queue (if configured) or remains in the failed state for inspection.
